meaning: n.鏀垮厷鏂归拡鐨勬紨璇翠汉
examples: For the first time, a woman will be the keynoter at the convention this year.
In 2011, we invited Linda Rising as the keynoter for her awesome talk about "Fearless Change" just after the earthquake disaster.
Quite a few attendees (and one keynoter) could and did give first-hand reports of the early days to others like myself who only discovered the power of descriptive markup from XML.
But Microsoft last year announced that it would no longer participate in the show, either as an exhibitor or a keynoter.
Instead, the party presented a more moderate message, just as the Republicans did when they chose Rep. Susan Molinari of New York as the GOP keynoter earlier this month.
